sectionSubtitle: Traditional feasts and meals, made from scratch

items.name: Tagine Feast
items.subtitle: Ras el hanout clay pot tradition
items.description: A communal tagine feast built around four ras el hanout tagines — two chickpea, one salmon, one chicken — all heavy on seasonal vegetables. Served family-style with sweet potatoes, cinnamon oranges, and mint tea.
dishes: Ras el hanout tagines (2 chickpea, 1 salmon, 1 chicken)
Sweet potatoes with smoked paprika yogurt/cashew sauce
Cinnamon spiced orange slices with pomegranate

steps: Season proteins and chickpeas with ras el hanout, garlic, ginger, saffron, and olive oil.
Layer onions and seasonal vegetables in tagine pots, nestle proteins on top.
Cover and slow-cook tagines until everything is tender and fragrant.
Roast sweet potatoes, serve with smoked paprika yogurt or cashew sauce.
Slice oranges, dust with cinnamon, scatter pomegranate seeds over the top.
Brew mint tea with gunpowder green tea, fresh spearmint, and sugar. Pour from height.

items.name: Couscous Feast
items.subtitle: Build-your-own couscous bowls
items.description: A choose-your-own couscous bowl bar with two bases, three proteins, and a spread of toppings and sauces — plus a dessert table and fresh-pressed juices.
dishes: Proteins: chicken · chickpeas (V) · lentil + sweet potato (V)
Toppings: cauliflower + carrot (V) · chermoula (V) · harissa (V) · yogurt · pickled radish + onion (V) · fresh herbs (V)
Dessert table: semolina cake · stuffed dates (V)
Drinks: pomegranate-mint-apple juice · ginger mint maple tea

steps: Steam couscous in a couscoussier — plain batch and herbed batch with parsley, cilantro, and olive oil.
Prepare proteins: roast chicken with ras el hanout, simmer chickpeas, cook lentil + sweet potato stew.
Roast cauliflower and carrots with cumin and turmeric.
Make chermoula and harissa sauces. Prepare pickled radish and onion quick-pickle.
Bake semolina cake with orange blossom water. Stuff dates with almond paste.
Press pomegranate-mint-apple juice. Brew ginger-mint-maple tea.
Set up bowl bar: bases first, then proteins, then toppings and sauces.

items.name: Kofta Feast
items.subtitle: Vegan kofta with eight sides
items.description: Vegan kofta and msemen flatbread anchoring a generous spread of eight Moroccan sides — from smoky zaalouk to crispy baked makouda — finished with orange blossom ice cream or sorbet.
dishes: Orange blossom ice cream · orange cream sorbet (V)

steps: Shape vegan kofta with spices, pan-sear or bake until browned.
Fold and griddle msemen flatbreads until golden and flaky.
Roast eggplant for zaalouk, char peppers for taktouka, roast beets.
Braise carrots with honey, cumin, and spices for khizou mcharmel.
Shape makouda patties, brush with oil, bake at 220°C until golden on both sides.
Roast cauliflower with cumin, paprika, and turmeric. Serve with chermoula.
Simmer bessara and loubia on the stovetop until thick and creamy.
Serve dessert: orange blossom ice cream (vegetarian) and orange cream sorbet (vegan).

items.name: Harira
items.subtitle: Bean-heavy Moroccan soup
items.description: Thick, hearty, bean-forward Moroccan soup. Navy beans, lentils, crushed tomatoes, and a deep bloom of cumin, coriander, ginger, and cinnamon. Gets significantly better overnight. Optional salmon poached in broth at service.

steps: Soak navy beans overnight, 8 hours minimum. Drain and rinse.
Build the base: heat olive oil, cook onions, celery, and garlic 12 minutes. Add tomato paste and stir until it darkens.
Bloom the spices: cumin, coriander, ginger, turmeric, paprika, cinnamon, black pepper, salt. Cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add beans and water, bring to a boil, simmer 45 minutes until beans begin to soften.
Add lentils and crushed tomatoes, simmer 30 more minutes until beans and lentils are completely tender.
Finish with cilantro, parsley, lemon juice, and olive oil. Refrigerate overnight.
Reheat gently, add water if too thick, re-season. Optional: poach salmon portions in simmering harira at service.

items.name: Zaalouk
items.subtitle: Creamy roasted eggplant dip
items.description: Deep smoky charred eggplant with generous argan oil. Part blended, part chunky — the guacamole moment. Gets dramatically better overnight.

steps: Char eggplants directly over flame or under a very hot broiler until skin is completely black and flesh has collapsed.
Peel, split open, and drain in a colander at least 20 minutes.
Cook garlic gently in olive oil, add cumin and paprika, then chopped tomatoes until jammy.
Add chopped eggplant, cook 15 minutes mashing until thick. Blend two-thirds smooth, fold back into the chunky third.
Off heat, stir in argan oil, preserved lemon, and lemon juice.
Fold in cilantro and parsley. Refrigerate overnight. Drizzle argan oil at service.

items.name: Beyond Merguez
items.subtitle: Homemade vegan sausages
items.description: Homemade vegan merguez with Beyond Meat. Heavy on spice and fat — sweet paprika, smoked paprika, harissa, fennel, and cumin. The protein star.

steps: Combine garlic, sweet paprika, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, crushed fennel seeds, cayenne, cinnamon, harissa, olive oil, salt and pepper into a spice paste.
Mix paste into cold Beyond Meat ground. Don't overmix.
Fry one small test patty — taste and adjust seasoning before forming the whole batch.
Form into small sausage shapes or patties, keep uniform for even cooking. Refrigerate until service.
Cook at high heat with olive oil, turning occasionally until browned on all sides — about 8 minutes per batch. Don't crowd the pan.

items.name: Roasted Zucchini
items.subtitle: Harissa and fresh mint
items.description: Roasted zucchini with harissa and fresh mint. Simple, fast, holds well. High heat for caramelized edges, not soft steamed zucchini.

steps: Cut zucchini into thick half-rounds, about half an inch. Too thin and they go mushy.
Toss with harissa, olive oil, cumin, and salt. Let sit 10 minutes.
Spread in a single layer across sheet trays — do not overlap. Roast at 425°F for 22 minutes, turning once.
Squeeze lemon juice over and scatter torn fresh mint just before serving.

items.name: Fennel & Orange Salad
items.subtitle: Shaved fennel with cumin dressing
items.description: Shaved fennel and navel orange with cumin dressing. The crunch and acid element. Traditional Moroccan, pre-Columbian.

steps: Shave fennel as thinly as possible (mandoline if you have one). Soak in cold water 10 minutes to crisp, drain well.
Peel oranges removing all pith, slice into rounds. Save any juice for the dressing.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, salt, pepper and reserved orange juice.
Toss fennel and orange with dressing just before serving. Add parsley and scatter fennel fronds. Serve within 30 minutes.

items.name: Pearl Barley
items.subtitle: Nutty, chewy neutral base
items.description: Pearl barley as the neutral base. Nutty, chewy, plain. Salt and olive oil only — keep it simple so everything else can shine.

steps: Rinse pearl barley in cold water until water runs clear.
Combine with water or light vegetable stock and salt. Bring to a boil, reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook 45 minutes until tender but still slightly chewy — should have some bite, not mushy.
Drain any excess liquid, stir in olive oil. Cover and keep warm until service.

items.name: Shiba Herbal Infusion
items.subtitle: Pre-colonial Amazigh tea ritual
items.description: Caffeine-free Amazigh herbal infusion — dried shiba wormwood, verbena louiza, and thyme steeped in boiling water. Unlike anything your guests have tasted. Pour from height for theater.

steps: Combine dried shiba wormwood, dried verbena louiza, and dried thyme in a teapot.
Pour boiling water over herbs. Steep 7 minutes — taste at 5 for gentle and floral, longer for more bitter and complex.
Strain into a serving vessel. Pour into small glasses from as high as you can manage. Slow and deliberate.
Serve immediately while hot. Small glasses — this is about ceremony and sipping.

items.name: Sellou
items.subtitle: Ancient Amazigh no-cook dessert
items.description: Toasted flour, almond, sesame, honey, argan oil, cinnamon. No cooking required — make ahead, it keeps for days and gets better. Serve in small portions alongside shiba tea.

steps: Toast flour in a large dry heavy pan over medium heat, stirring constantly 20 minutes until golden brown and nutty. Do not stop stirring.
Toast almond flour over medium-low heat, stirring constantly 7 minutes until golden. It burns faster — watch carefully.
Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan, stirring 4 minutes until golden and fragrant. They will pop when ready.
Combine all toasted ingredients with cinnamon, ground anise, salt, and powdered sugar.
Warm honey so it flows. Pour honey, argan oil, and olive oil over the dry mixture. Mix with your hands — crumbly but holds its shape when pressed.
Taste and adjust. Pack into a sealed container. At service, mound in small bowls with a dusting of powdered sugar and cinnamon.

categories.name: Moroccan Cafe Food
categories.teaserText: Moroccan cafe bites coming soon — think Moroccan-chicken pizza, crispy briouats, and more. Stay tuned.
